checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Revenues grew only from 116,645,000 (FY2010) to 145,898,000 (FY2015), a 1.25x increase
Goodwill=11,365,000 (2015-12-31) jumped to 76,833,000 (2016-03-31) coincident with a large acquisition (Assets roughly doubled from 181,179,000 to 297,157,000 in the same quarter)
evidence.location: 10-Q Q1 2016 balance sheet, Goodwill and Assets

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=16,751,000 (FY2011) vs NetIncomeLoss=7,371,000 (FY2011); OCF=18,990,000 (FY2012) vs NI=7,946,000 (FY2012); OCF=11,442,000 (FY2015) vs NI=4,005,000 (FY2015)
evidence.location: 10-K FY2015 cash flow and income statements

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: Revenues grew only 25% over the same period, from 116,645,000 (FY2010) to 145,898,000 (FY2015)
GrossProfit/Revenues margin declined slightly from 64.6% (FY2012, GrossProfit=85,018,000/Revenues=131,718,000) to 62.2% (FY2015, GrossProfit=90,719,000/Revenues=145,898,000)

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=3,944,000 (FY2010); 7,371,000 (FY2011); 7,946,000 (FY2012); 16,172,000 (FY2013); 7,322,000 (FY2014); 4,005,000 (FY2015) -- substantial year-to-year volatility including a large 2013 spike and 2015 trough
Quarterly NetIncomeLoss swung from -274,000 (Q1 2015) to -502,000 (Q2 2015) to 2,145,000 (Q3 2015)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AccruedLiabilitiesCurrent=5,131,000 (2011-12-31) declined to 2,411,000 (2013-12-31) even as revenue grew from 119,626,000 (FY2011) to 140,763,000 (FY2013)
DeferredRevenueCurrent=1,401,000 (2012-12-31) fell sharply to 316,000 (2013-12-31), a 77% decline, while revenue grew 6.9% in the same year

NetIncomeLoss for Q4 2013 alone was 9,026,000, far above the typical quarterly run-rate of 2,000-3,000 thousand seen in adjacent quarters, coincident with the reserve/deferred-revenue declines noted above

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2016-03-25, roughly five weeks after the original 10-K filed 2016-02-16 (accession 0001193125-16-465362)
Multiple CORRESP filings with the SEC in 2015-2016 (e.g., 2016-03-15, 2016-03-25) indicating SEC comment-letter activity around the FY2015 10-K period

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data provided for Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, InventoryNet, and cash flow items spanning FY2009 through Q3 2016

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible premature or accelerated revenue recognition: receivables grew far faster than revenue in 2013-2015 (AR +48.5% vs revenue +22% over the period) while deferred revenue simultaneously collapsed (from 1,401,000 in FY2012 to 316,000 in FY2013), consistent with revenue being recognized earlier than cash/performance obligations were satisfied, inflating reported revenue and receivables while shrinking the offsetting deferred-revenue liability.

Possible reserve/accrual release used to boost reported earnings: accrued liabilities fell from 5,131,000 (2011) to 2,411,000 (2013) even as revenue grew, and Q4 2013 net income spiked to 9,026,000 versus a typical 2,000-3,000 (thousand) quarterly run-rate, suggesting a one-time reduction in accrued liabilities/reserves flowed through income rather than reflecting a genuine change in underlying obligations.

Possible deferred inventory write-downs/obsolescence reserves: inventory grew 128% from 2010 to 2015 while revenue grew only 25% over the same period, suggesting inventory (much of it tissue/bio-implant products with shelf-life risk) may not be adequately reserved for obsolescence, which would understate cost of goods sold and overstate gross margin/inventory asset values.

top_signals: Receivables grew ~48.5% (2013-2015) vs revenue growth of ~22% over the same period, indicating potential revenue-recognition timing issues
Deferred revenue collapsed 77% in FY2013 while accrued liabilities simultaneously fell from 5,131,000 (2011) to 2,411,000 (2013), coincident with an anomalous 9,026,000 Q4 2013 net income spike
Inventory grew 128% from 2010-2015 versus only 25% revenue growth, raising obsolescence/reserve-adequacy concerns
A 10-K/A amendment was filed only ~5 weeks after the FY2015 10-K, alongside multiple SEC CORRESP filings in the same window
